title: Family Cell Pizza Workshop
description: Recommended for ages 7-12 What makes a plant cell different from an animal cell? Young chefs, step into the kitchen at A Tavola Pizzeria with Professor Dan Lloyd from the University of Kent for a deliciously fun science workshop. Make some "cells" (pizzas) and then eat them for lunch! Supported by A Tavola Pizzeria Restaurant
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Agatha Christie: On Screen, Stage & Page
description: What is it about Agatha Christie’s novels and plays that makes them so brilliant and relevant years after her death? Christie super-fan and author Cathy Rentzenbrink is joined by theatre historian Julius Green and screenwriter Sarah Phelps ( EastEnders , BBC adaptations And Then There Were None, The Witness for the Prosecution and The ABC Murders ) for a celebration of Agatha and her work across screen, stage and page.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Alistair Moffat: Assassins and Secret Listeners
description: Historian, writer and former director of the Edinburgh Festival Fringe, Alistair Moffat, tells the half-forgotten stories of ordinary men and women here in Kent and around the UK who found their own ways to fight against the Nazis from home. From listening to coded messages and booby trapping Charlton Park in the event of an invasion, to joining auxiliary units that were poised to undertake suicidal missions and assassinations, he shares stories of immense bravery and finally pays these heroes their due.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Canterbury: City of Sanctuary
description: As Canterbury district strives for “City of Sanctuary” status, discover how local leaders are building on the district’s long and proud history of welcoming those fleeing violence, conflict and persecution. With former University of Kent Vice Chancellor, Georgina Randsley de Moura and The Very Reverend Dean David Monteith of Canterbury Cathedral. Chaired by Prof Becky Huxley-Binns Vice Chancellor, Canterbury Christ Church University.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Caroline Quentin: Drawn To Nature
description: Actress and passionate life-long gardener Caroline Quentin invites you to discover how to find joy and calm in the natural world, wherever you live and whatever your space. Join us for an intimate conversation with Caroline which brings together stories from her life as an actor with practical wisdom for a greener calmer life, to celebrate the places, people and gardens that shaped her love of the outdoors.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Craig Revel Horwood: The Book of Panto
description: You might expect, after twenty years performing in pantomime, that Craig Revel Horwood has always been a fan of Panto, but that’s not the case! Craig comes to Canterbury, the spiritual home of panto in Kent, to discuss his new book, The Book of Panto. Learn about the moments that turned him from a sceptic to a wholehearted fan of – and advocate for – panto, as he follows the art form’s journey from its international roots to its current status as a beloved British phenomenon.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Dan Jones: Castles
description: Discover a fortified history of the world told through over three thousand years of iconic strongholds with bestselling author of The Templars and Powers and Thrones , Dan Jones. Castles have been built to defend humanity for millennia - from the walls of Troy and the desert bastion of Krak des Chevaliers in Syria; to Windsor Castle and the Tower of London; to Himeji in Japan and the Maginot Line in the Second World War. Take a journey through centuries and across continents as Jones reveals an epic story of how humans first came to build fortifications and how they expanded into powerfully capturing our imagination.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: David Shrigley: Creative Writing
description: Master of the inexplicable and surreal, Turner Prize nominee, and Instagram phenomenon David Shrigley takes to the stage to discuss his comic writing, presented – outrageously?! – in a big new book with no illustrations. Enjoy an evening with a cultural icon in pure words, pure joy, pure Shrigley.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Eleanor Drage: What If We Got AI Right?
description: In a world obsessed with whether AI will destroy humanity, Eleanor Drage, one of the world’s leading AI ethicists, is asking a far more urgent — and actionable — question: what if we got AI right? Join Eleanor in conversation as she reframes the dominant narratives around artificial intelligence as a social, political and ethical issue — not just a technical one, offering a radically more hopeful, human-centred vision.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Elif Shafak: In One Brief Moment All Eternity
description: Join multi-award-winning British-Turkish novelist and storyteller Elif Shafak as she discusses her new novel, In One Brief Moment All Eternity , a meditation on creativity in the darkest of times. In this boldly rendered novel, Elif Shafak imagines in vivid colour the life of legendary French writer Gustave Flaubert and all the women who possessed him—the fictional ones he conjured and the real ones who shaped him—particularly Madame Bovary. On the heels of her most successful book yet, There Are Rivers in the Sky , Shafak gives us a triumphant new tale that weaves the traditions of the European realist novel with the ethereal, twilight-tinged oral storytelling of the Middle East.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Giulia Enders: Organ Speak
description: The international bestselling author of Gut and star of Netflix’s Hack Your Health invites us on a spellbinding voyage through the human body from her perspective of one of the world's leading researchers in the area, introducing us to its unsung heroes helping us to thrive – from muscles and immune systems to brains and emotions. What does it really mean to listen to our bodies?
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Harry Baker: Tender
description: World Poetry Slam Champion and bestselling poet Harry Baker brings his characteristic warmth, wit and wonder to new parenthood in this evening of spoken word. Tender contains poems from the first hundred days of new life and celebrates all of the euphoria, panic and vulnerability that come with it. One of the UK’s most magnetic performers, Harry is building on the last two years’ 40-date sellout runs of Wonderful and Wonderful 2.0 , and will even slip in some non-baby bangers too. Expect more laughter and tears than ever.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Hugo Max: Nosferatu (1922) with Live Score
description: Join Hugo Max as he accompanies one of the greatest horror films of the silent era with a live soundtrack performed on solo viola, piano and an ever-changing variety of smaller instruments. One of the most famous of all silent movies, F. W. Murnau’s Nosferatu continues to haunt modern audiences. Hugo Max has presented nationwide tours focusing on silent horror, expressionism, the films of F. W. Murnau and Alfred Hitchcock, making solo appearances at many of the country's landmark venues including the BFI Southbank, Bo'ness' Hippodrome and Tyneside Cinema.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Jackie Eales: British Historic Town Atlas of Canterbury
description: Professor Jackie Eales, President of the British Association for Local History introduces the N ew Historical Atlas of Canterbury . In this talk, Jackie presents maps of the city across different historical periods, offers a concise history of Canterbury from Roman times to the present, and explores an A–Z guide to its most significant historic buildings.  She also shares insights on how the Atlas was compiled, highlights some lesser-known sources of Canterbury history and shares new discoveries made by the editorial team.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Jay Rayner: Nights Out in the Kitchen
description: Award-winning writer, musician, journalist and broadcaster Jay Rayner reflects on more than 25 years as a restaurant critic. To celebrate his new book, Nights Out in the Kitchen , Jay considers everything from the problem with dinner parties, through the lexicographical challenges facing a restaurant critic who wants to describe flavour without resorting to ‘mouth-watering’, ‘moist’ and ‘sumptuous’, to the burning question of whether, given the large number of wretchedly negative reviews he’s written, Jay might actually be a total scumbag. Feast on Jay’s love of restaurants and his infectious passion for great home cooking.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Jo Baring: The Untold Story of Sculpture
description: For forty thousand years, we have been carving and moulding, shaping and chiselling, feeling our way and sensing our place in the world through sculpture. In this accessible, story-led journey into the heart of sculpture, renowned art historian and curator Jo Baring explores creativity, memory and why we have been driven to make art in this medium since the very beginning of humanity.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Maggie Aderin: Secrets of the Cosmos
description: Sky at Night Presenter Dr Maggie Aderin takes you on a deep exploration to the very beginning of the universe, including the first galaxies, stars and exoplanets as seen through the lens of James Webb Space Telescope (JWST). Since 2022 the JWST has been sending back stunning images, and the study of these has changed the way scientists see many aspects of the universe. In her new book, James Webb’s Ancient Cosmos , A Journey to the Beginning of Time , Dr Maggie helps to illuminate the main findings, taking readers on a journey through space and back to the cosmic dawn – the very beginning of time itself.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Malcolm Gaskill: Worlds of Fear & Wonder
description: Malcolm Gaskill (author of Witchfinders, The Glass Mountain and the Sunday Times -bestselling The Ruin of All Witches ) examines our haunted past from alchemical dens to witchcraft trials to unlock the secrets of the unseen world, opening a new perspective on the magical and marvellous.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Mary Portas: I Shop, Therefore I Am
description: Businesswoman, broadcaster, author and activist Mary Portas reveals how, armed only with the blank canvas of a Harvey Nicks shop window and her own creativity, she created a business empire that has stood the test of time. In conversation for Canterbury Festival, Mary Portas looks back on the 90s and takes us behind the shop window – from behind-the-scenes fashion gossip to the early lessons that shaped her career. Join us for a celebration of vision, risk, and business as a creative act.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Max Adams: Domesday
description: Join author and archaeologist Max Adams as he re-treads the path taken in 1085 by King William I’s agents, taking us on foot through the very sites covered by one of the most iconic documents of English history. He reveals a unique portrait of a land and its people, bringing the past into intimate contact with the present — evoking eleventh-century England through contemporary eyes with the original account as his guide.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Nicholas Orme: Chaucer's England
description: Who were Chaucer’s thirty-three pilgrims? Why were they heading to Canterbury? And what do they and their tales tell us about medieval England? Leading historian Nicholas Orme brings literature and history together, using the Canterbury Tales and the other great writings of Chaucer’s day to explain how people truly lived, what they thought about time and space, religion, politics and the landscape they lived in.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Nikesh Shukla: 10 Years of The Good Immigrant
description: Ten years ago, The Good Immigrant essay collection was crowdfunded in three days, declared a Sunday Times bestseller, and described by Zadie Smith as ‘a must-read’. The publishing industry had said no. The public said otherwise.  Now, to mark the 10th anniversary of this pivotal book, editor Nikesh Shukla reflects on what’s changed in Britain in the decade since publication and what hasn’t.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Sathnam Sanghera: The Meaning of George Michael
description: Discover George Michael through the eyes of bestselling author and historian Sathnam Sanghera, author of Empireland , as he paints a portrait of one of Britain’s most beloved musicians over the course of a strange and turbulent period of British history. In conversation about his unconventional and enthralling new book, Tonight The Music Seems So Loud , Sathnam explores the connection between music and politics and exposes what secrecy does to the soul, all while capturing, joyfully and poignantly, one of Britain’s greatest artists in all his musical glory.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Sebastian Barry: The Newer World
description: Twice Costa Book of the Year winner and 'the most miraculous writer' (Russell T Davies), Sebastian Barry joins us to celebrate his new novel, The Newer World . Set in the aftermath of the American Civil War, this lyrical, visceral novel follows the life of Tennyson Bouguereau to ask what it is to survive, and what might be lost along the way. ‘A masterly work of historical witness and moral reckoning.’ (Observer, Fiction to look out for in 2026)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Simon Armitage: Gilgamesh
description: The Poet Laureate performs his exhilarating new verse translation of the ancient epic poem Gilgamesh in the finale of this year's Books & Ideas programme. Gilgamesh originated in Ancient Mesopotamia nearly 4,000 years ago, yet its themes of power, loyalty, love and loss resound with contemporary relevance. Simon Armitage’s fresh and propulsive version brings Gilgamesh alive as poetry, transporting us back to the very origins of civilisation and storytelling.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Simon Garfield: The Pen
description: Journey into the history of writing, thought, and culture through the story of the pen and its integral role in shaping our lives into the twenty-first century with New York Times bestselling author Simon Garfield ( Mauve, Just My Type and On The Map .)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Simon Sebag Montefiore: The Cauldron
description: Explore the making of the modern Middle East with bestselling, prizewinning historian and master storyteller, Simon Sebag Montefiore. The Middle East has long been the source of religions, prize of empires, spark-wheel of conflicts, well of radicalism and epicentre of our tumultuous world. Yet few know its real history; fewer still understand its variety and cosmopolitanism, its complexity and interconnectedness. Join him in conversation as he considers how the Middle East of 1900 became the Middle East of today – and tomorrow. Tickets including a signed hardback of The Cauldron: The Making of the Modern Middle East are available.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Spies: From Fact to Fiction
description: Pry into the history of the real 20th-century British spies whose lives continue to inspire writers today. In Stalin’s Apostles , writer, critic and journalist Antonia Senior uses declassified files to unpick the treachery of Stalin’s famous “Cambridge Five” spies. In A Stranger in Corfu , prize-winning author Alex Preston creates a vivid reimagining of a real, hidden slice of the British Intelligence Service’s history, an island where former members of MI6 are sent to be forgotten. Join them in conversation as they explore historical facts that seem stranger than fiction.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Swifts: From the Congo to Kent and Back Again
description: Join Edward Mayer, founder of Swift Conservation, as he tells of his remarkable journey to protect the UK’s Common Swift. From childhood fascination to working with the RSPB, Edward shares how citizen action, architecture and collaboration can transform urban habitats for these extraordinary birds. Discover the challenges Swifts face, the innovative solutions that safeguard their nesting sites, and how local communities across Britain and Europe are making a difference, including here in Kent.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Terry Deary: History of the World in Ten Wars
description: A lively, irreverent exploration of the ten wars that shaped world history and what they reveal about human nature. With his trademark humour, Terry Deary (author of Revolting, A History of Britain in Ten Enemies and the Horrible Histories series) moves from ancient Greece to the twentieth century, uncovering unexpected stories, myths and turning points often missed in traditional accounts. Rather than glorifying battle, Deary asks: why do we keep fighting? How can societies justify conflict? And can anything truly be learned from it?
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: The Full English: The Ways We Eat and Why
description: Explore the deep roots of English food culture and the remarkable connections between food and power, class and culture, tradition and change via eight iconic foodstuffs including olive oil, Greggs sausage rolls, roast beef, and curry (naturally). Discover the hidden forces shaping the nation’s food, its dining habits, and its people with award-winning podcaster Lewis Bassett, a chef in search of an answer.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: William Boyd: Cold Sunset
description: An evening with acclaimed author William Boyd celebrating the latest novel in his Sunday Times bestselling espionage series starring Gabriel Dax: travel writer, reluctant spy and accidental hero. The new stylish mystery takes us deep into a shadow-drenched world where loyalty blurs, motives darken and every step could be fatal. Enter Cold War Moscow and discover a place smouldering with secrets, as told by one of the UK’s most beloved novelists.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Wine: A Global History
description: Savour the untold story of wine from the stone age to the present day as acclaimed historian Kathleen Burk speaks to local wine expert Clive Barlow, about the history of humanity’s favourite corked beverage. From the planting of grape seeds in conquered territories across Ancient Rome, to the uprooting of vineyards during the Chinese Communist Revolution, wine has been used to celebrate and commiserate, as a negotiating tool and a diplomatic gift across millennia. But where - and when - was it first discovered? And how did it become a worldwide phenomenon?
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine Hall - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: Poetry Showcase
description: Published poets from Kent and Sussex come together at this year’s Festival with readings of their work followed by a short open mic set. Poets include John McCullough, Setareh Ebrahimi, Bethany Goodwill, Carolyn Oulton, David Dykes, Mat Ritches Goodwill, Carolyn Oulton and David Dykes.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA
venue_name: Augustine House - Canterbury Christ Church University

title: The Good Gnus
description: Summer in Barham returns to the beautifully intimate Barham Church this July with an evening of musical mischief from Canterbury favourites, The Good Gnus. Dr Jon Williams and Dr Chris Price—two singers who once shared not just a choir stall in Canterbury Cathedral, but also a love of music that would never have echoed through its venerable vaults—bring their playful spirit to Barham for one night only. Joined by their talented friend and fellow musician, the dexterous Mr David Smith, the trio share their rediscovered gems from some of the greatest musical humourists of the last century. What began with the songs of Flanders and Swann has grown into a repertoire that now includes Noël Coward, Richard Stilgoe, Peter Skellern, Jake Thackray and Tom Lehrer. Together they explore everything from love and romance to ecclesiastical curiosities, national affairs, and the ever changing economy—though Flanders and Swann's original Budget Song now requires a rewrite for every performance, a testament to the group’s dedication to freshness and topical wit. Favourites such as The Gasman Comet h and Joyce the Librarian usually make an appearance, but the trio has one requirement: that their audiences should be prepared to roar the chorus of The Hippopotamus Song with as much gusto as is humanly possible. As Chris notes, “We love the quicksilver wit and deft composition found in these songs, and their appeal is timeless. They’re also surprisingly topical.” The trio look forward to bringing their customary joie de vivre to the Summer in Barham weekend, promising an uplifting and thoroughly entertaining evening for all. The church will be running a small bar with wine and light refreshments for a donation, with all proceeds going to help the church. St John the Baptist's Church, Church Ln, Barham, Canterbury CT4 6PB
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Mampama Quintet – supported by Gombo
description: Join the infectiously rhythmic Mampama Quintet for a vibrant afternoon of live music in the stunning grounds of Barham Court. Bring a picnic, settle in on the green, and don’t forget your dancing shoes! The Mampama Quintet brings together talented musicians from Kent and London to celebrate two iconic West African musical traditions: the lilting, fingerpicked melodies of Sierra Leonean Palm Wine music and the infectious rhythms of Ghanaian Highlife. These timeless genres have shaped artists worldwide, and the Quintet honours their rich heritage with performances of classic tracks by E.T. Mensah, S.E. Rogie, Osibisa, and more. Mampama Quintet are: Simon Lee Guitar — Vocals Kevin Richards — Acoustic Guitar Vocals Kofi Adu — Drums Henri Gaobi — Percussion Emmanuel Darko — Bass guitar Our Canterbury Festival 2025 Contraband winners Gombo warm things up for the Mampama Quintet. Featuring students of both Simon Langton Grammar School for Boys and Girls, Gombo astounded judges at our 2025 Contraband showcase winning a cash prize to support their musical journey. We are incredibly excited to showcase this talented young band once again and can't wait to see them live at Barham Court. Barham Court, Rectory Lane, Barham, Canterbury, CT4 6PD Parking is available on-site. Please note this is an outdoor picnic event - please bring blankets and camping chairs. There is no wet weather venue, so please do bring an umbrella just in case! Regretfully due to the layout and architecture of the building and garden, this venue is not wheelchair friendly. Please contact the box office on 01227 457568 for more information.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: The Red Stripe Band
description: Our final day of Summer in Barham presents The Red Stripe Band , guaranteed to get things moving with their energetic and engaging blend of jive, rock n roll and boogie woogie classics. Led by Red Stripe on boogie piano the band feature a star studded line-up with singer Lara de Belder (All Female London Gay Big Band), on bass Costa Tancredi (Eric Clapton, Amy Winehouse) on Baritone Sax Sim Murray (The Beat) on Tenor Sax Sophie Burke, on Trumpet Lee Vivian (Suzi Quatro, Will Young, Nick Heyward) and on Drums Nick Marangoni (Massimo Ranieri). The band have played at almost every Jazz and Blues Festival in the land including a memorable show at Montreux Jazz Festival. Celebrating thirty years in the business, they recently released their fifth album. Their influences range from the big bands and the jive giants of the 1940s to the pounding piano men of the 1950s with a touch of ska for good measure. Barham Court, Rectory Lane, Barham, Canterbury, CT4 6PD Gates will open at 4pm with music starting at 5pm. The event will end at approx. 6.50pm, leaving plenty of time for attendees to make it home in time for the World Cup Final. Parking is available on-site. Please note this is an outdoor picnic event - please bring blankets and camping chairs. There is no wet weather venue, so please do bring an umbrella just in case! Regretfully due to the layout and architecture of the building and garden, this venue is not wheelchair friendly. Please contact the box office on 01227 457568 for more information.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Bach To Baby: Gulliver's Travels
description: Tickets £16.50 (Each adult ticket includes free entry for up to 2 children) Aimed at ages 0 – 5, all welcome Ahoy, explorers! Set sail on a literary classic brought to life with music, movement, and imagination. Join Gulliver on his epic adventure – dance to lively sea shanties, twirl to Telemann’s fiddles, and encounter giants, Lilliputians, and dreamy Laputans along the way! Bach to Baby is the critically-acclaimed concert series for babies, toddlers and you. Tots dance, roam about and revel in the wonder of live music, while you take a moment out of your busy day to hear top notch performances by outstanding musicians. As seen at Royal Albert Hall and Buckingham Palace.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Bach To Baby: Gulliver's Travels
description: Tickets £16.50 (Each adult ticket includes free entry for up to 2 children) Aimed at ages 0 – 5, all welcome Ahoy, explorers! Set sail on a literary classic brought to life with music, movement, and imagination. Join Gulliver on his epic adventure – dance to lively sea shanties, twirl to Telemann’s fiddles, and encounter giants, Lilliputians, and dreamy Laputans along the way! Bach to Baby is the critically-acclaimed concert series for babies, toddlers and you. Tots dance, roam about and revel in the wonder of live music, while you take a moment out of your busy day to hear top notch performances by outstanding musicians. As seen at Royal Albert Hall and Buckingham Palace.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Playground - Baby Playground
description: Free, For babies 0 – 18 months Baby Playground creates a magical experience for you to explore with your baby. From exploring music, light, textures and sounds to movement and smells, these exciting sessions are a perfect opportunity for families to engage with new experiences, sensations and connect with each other.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Playground - Specialist Session (SEND)
description: For ages 0 - 5 years The Playground Specialist Session (1.30pm) presents free creative and multi-sensory activities especially designed for children, parents/carers, teachers and support staff in Special Education Needs and Disabilities (SEND) settings supported by artists from a range of disciplines, including music, visual arts, drama and dance.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Anna Lapwood: Live in Canterbury Cathedral
description: One of the UK’s most dynamic ambassadors for classical music gives her first performance on the Canterbury Cathedral Organ. Expect a dazzling programme of film music interspersed with traditional organ repertoire. As the official organist of the Royal Albert Hall, Anna Lapwood has broken genre boundaries and introduced a huge new audience to the organ. An established conductor, broadcaster, and a fierce advocate for the advancement of female voices within her industry, she has reached millions via her devoted social media following and collaborated with artists from Florence & the Machine to Benedict Cumberbatch. A live video feed of Anna’s performance will be projected into the central nave.
address: Canterbury Cathedral, The Precincts, The King's Mile

title: APOLLO5: Crossings
description: Take a musical pilgrimage along the Via Francigena as critically acclaimed British vocal ensemble APOLLO5 launch their new album Crossings at Canterbury Festival with an expansive programme which maps this historic pilgrimage route in song. One of Britains’ smallest but most dynamic vocal groups bring their vibrant and versatile sound to the atmospheric setting of Canterbury Cathedral. Part of the VOCES8 Foundation, APOLLO5’s repertoire embraces Renaissance, classical and contemporary works alongside folk, jazz and pop. Crossings traces the pilgrimage route of the Via Francigena from Canterbury to Rome, threading sacred and secular music across centuries and borders in a programme which includes The Last Rose as well as English art songs, a tender Irish lament and close harmony jazz standards that bloom into radiant arrangements of much-loved pop songs by Seal and Bill Withers. Part 1: Crossings Thomas Morley - Arise, Awake Robert White Christe - qui lux es et dies Piers Connor Kennedy - Strike it up, Tabor Shruthi Rajasekar - Who Has Seen the Wind? Tim Laycock arr. Penelope Appleyard - Row On Claude de Jeune Revecy - venir du printans Francis Pott - Méditation Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina - Sanctus from Missa Regina Caeli Vicente Lusitano Regina - caeli laetare INTERVAL Part 2: The Last Rose Ralph Vaughan Williams arr. Harry D Bennett- The Call Benjamin Britten - The Evening Primrose Benjamin Britten - The Ballad Of Green Broom Trad. Irish arr. Fraser Wilson - The Last Rose Of Summer Manning Sherwin & Eric Maschwitz arr. Jim Clements - A Nightingale Sang In Berkeley Square Jack Strachey & Eric Maschwitz arr. Jim Clements - These Foolish Things Seal arr. Blake Morgan - Kiss From A Rose Bill Withers arr. Blake Morgan - Lovely Day
address: Canterbury Cathedral, The Precincts, The King's Mile

title: Britten Sinfonia's Surround Sound Playlist
description: Lie down and discover a completely new way to experience world-class classical music in this relaxed, immersive performance with Britten Sinfonia and Tenebrae in the magnificent setting of the Canterbury Cathedral Nave. You’re sitting or lying on mats in the Cathedral, looking up at the soaring arches and beautiful ceiling above. Lighting states shift and capture the mood as a seamless musical sequence fills the magical space from all directions... like a drivetime radio mix or eclectic playlist, but live and all around you, performed by more than 100 musicians, with orchestral drama, solo virtuosity and heart bursting moments of choral beauty. From the beloved and instantly recognisable to add-to-playlist new favourites, enjoy orchestral, choral, solo and chamber music by Allegri, Bartok, Copland, Ēriks Ešenvalds, Grieg, Handel, Morten Lauridsen, Parry, Prokofiev, Ravel, Caroline Shaw, Telemann and many more. Whether you love classical music or you’ve never thought it was for you, Surround Sound Playlist offers an inspiring and unforgettable new experience. Enable new audiences to experience these exceptional artists by selecting the price you can afford. Premium Seats: Choose from a select number of seats with excellent views of all stages. Listening Seats: Take a seat in one of the side aisles looking into the centre and listen to the full performance, with a partial or intermittent view of the performers as they move around.
address: Canterbury Cathedral, The Precincts, The King's Mile

title: Canterbury Cathedral presents Messiaen, Livre du Saint Sacrement (1984), played by Tom Bell
description: Messiaen, Livre du Saint Sacrement (1984) - Organ Recital by Tom Bell, Concert Organist This event is organised, managed and delivered by Canterbury Cathedral. Renowned concert organist Tom Bell will play the Livre du Saint Sacrement, written in 1984 by Olivier Messiaen (1908 to 1992). Over 100 minutes long, the Livre due Saint Sacrement is a vast cycle in eighteen pieces, collectively presenting a meditation on the Blessed Sacrement: the body and blood of Jesus, in the form of bread and wine at the Eucharist (Holy Communion). Each individual piece is a meditation on its title, and the text(s) Messiaen appends to it. These texts come from a variety of sources: the Bible, Catholic tradition, ancient and more recent theologians. Tom Bell's playing has been described as "invigorating" (Sunday Times), "compelling" (Organists' Review) and "brilliant" (American Record Guide). He has performed across Europe, in Australia, the Far East and the USA; his recordings have been broadcast and won praise internationally. His album Freak Out! (Regent 2021), was described in The American Organist as “remarkable…a masterclass”, and in BBC Music Magazine as “in equal parts brilliant and a bit bonkers”. “Beyond dispute”, continues The American Organist, “is Bell’s grand vision of organ music.” Tom has collaborated with poets, dancers, visual artists and folk musicians. He has premiered many new works including chorale preludes for the Orgelbüchlein Project and a cantata by Divine Comedy frontman Neil Hannon. Tom pioneered the combination of organ and beatbox, performing with Shlomo on BBC Radio 3 and at the Royal Festival Hall. In May 2019 Tom participated in the first complete performance of the Orgelbüchlein Project at the Orgelpark in Amsterdam. The 164 pieces took four organists, and five organs, nine hours to perform. A similar marathon in London in 2023 received international press attention. Tom participated in a performance of Jean Guillou's nine-organ epic, La Révolte des Orgues, at Westminster Cathedral; he returned to that place in April 2022 to perform Olivier Messiaen’s last and longest organ work Livre du Saint Sacrement on the 30th anniversary of the composer’s death. He has performed the Livre on multiple occasions at venues including: Three Choirs Festival (2024, Pershore Abbey); Edinburgh Fringe (2024, St. Giles Cathedral); Salisbury Cathedral (2025); Konstantin-Basilika, Trier, Germany (2025). In 2026 performances will take place at Liverpool Metropolitan Cathedral, Buckfast Abbey and Canterbury Cathedral. Tom has a Messiaen vlog on YouTube. His film project with Fugue State Films (Messiaen’s Musical Universe, released in 2024) has been widely praised. Organists’ Review said of it that “Bell plays with clarity, passion, and always with a clear sense of thought… The music simply pours from him, as if we are hearing it fresh and clean without the unwanted weight of a performer feeling the need to impose their own personality. Bell’s focus is pure and powerful”. Another current interest concerns a study of English nineteenth century performance practice, particularly the work of W. T. Best. This project encompasses academic writings, and pure fun in his costumed quasi-steampunk The Best Experience concerts. Tom’s students have held prestigious positions across the UK. Organist to the renowned Organworks project from 2007-2017, he continues his educational work as a Director of the RCO. Tom was Artistic Director of the London Organ Day 2013-2026, is a patron of the Society of Women Organists and a member of the Association of Independent Organ Advisers. He was taught by Kevin Bowyer, Jacques van Oortmerssen and Ann Elise Smoot. Tom’s main focus at present is being a Dad to two young children, but he remains active as a musician (albeit functioning on a bit less sleep than previously!). tombell-organist.net
address: Canterbury Cathedral, The Precincts, The King's Mile

title: Canterbury Choral Society: Festivities, Fanfare and Fireworks
description: Handel Coronation Anthems The King Shall Rejoice Let Thy Hand be Strengthened Handel - Music for the Royal Fireworks Haydn - Harmoniemesse Soprano - Anna-Luise Wagner Alto - Meriel Cunningham Tenor - Nicholas Madden Bass - Benjamin Bevan The London Mozart Players Join Canterbury Choral Society with the London Mozart Players for an evening of celebratory masterpieces in the beauty of the Canterbury Cathedral Nave. Bringing together the glory of the high baroque and classical periods, this concert brings two of Handel’s majestic anthems written for the Coronation of King George 2nd in 1727 together with the music he wrote for a grand fireworks display following a victory against the French. This is followed by one of the finest of Haydn’s six great masses, his Harmoniemesse , written for the birthday of his employer’s wife in Esterhazy, Princess Maria.
address: Canterbury Cathedral, The Precincts, The King's Mile

title: Circa: Eternity
description: A one-of-a-kind Festival experience. Acrobats hang from the roof of the Nave and perform incredible feats in a bespoke Cathedral circus piece for a rare UK performance. Step into Canterbury Cathedral for a transformative hour of world class circus from Australian ensemble, Circa. Eternity has been created by visionary Yaron Lifschitz and is performed to a score of contemporary spiritual music by Arvo Pärt: Tabula Rasa and Frates , recorded by the Australian Chamber Orchestra. Take your seat around the catwalk stage to witness these talented circus artists explore powerful emotions and delve into profound themes such as love, death, loss, hope and wonder. Eternity is co-commissioned by Brisbane Festival and City of Newcastle’s New Annual festival Circa acknowledges the assistance of the Australian Government through Creative Australia, its principal arts investment and advisory body and the Queensland Government through Arts Queensland. ‘The setting is sublime, the music is glorious, the skills are impressive, and the whole show is an absolute delight to watch.’ (Nothing Ever Happens in Brisbane) Advised for ages 10+.
address: Canterbury Cathedral, The Precincts, The King's Mile

title: Adam Kay: This is Going to Hurt
description: BAFTA-winning comedian Adam Kay shares his uniquely hilarious diaries as a junior doctor in this ‘electrifying’ (Guardian) evening of stand-up. Based on the multi-million selling book and multi-award-winning TV series, This is Going to Hurt ‘intersperses horror stories from the NHS frontline with a catalogue of sublimely silly spoof songs, and some blissfully brilliant wordplay’ ( Mail on Sunday ) in a comedy performance that has delighted over 350,000 people. Plus a support act to be announced. ‘Hilarious and heartbreaking.’ (Charlie Brooker) Ages 16+, contains medical themes and stories that some may find distressing.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Braimah Kanneh-Mason and Sophia Rahman
description: Bologne - Violin sonata G minor Op.1 Arvo Pärt - Fratres Boulanger - Two Pieces for Violin and Piano, Nocturne Korngold - Marietta’s Lied from Die Tote Stadt op. 12 Shostakovich - The Gadfly (Ovod): Suite, op.97a: Romance Price - Adoration (solo violin & orchestra) Mozart - Sonata for Violin and Piano in E minor KV 304 Grieg - Sonata No.3 for Violin and Piano in C minor, Op. 45 Violinist Braimah Kanneh-Mason is joined by pianist Sophia Rahman for this Canterbury Festival performance, presenting together a varied programme of works including pieces by Pärt, Shostakovich, Mozart and more. The second eldest of the Kanneh-Mason siblings, Braimah Kanneh-Mason is a dynamic and engaging violinist who has performed throughout the UK and internationally in venues such as London’s Wigmore Hall and Barbican Hall. Known for her ‘supreme chamber-musical responsiveness’ (The Arts Desk) Sophia recently played the upright piano in Matthew Bourne’s The Red Shoes at Sadler’s Wells in 2026 and is Artistic Director of the Whittington Festival.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Celia Imrie in Conversation
description: Join national treasure, Celia Imrie, in conversation with her longtime friend and travel companion, Fidelis Morgan, as she journeys back in time to share the most memorable adventures of her life and celebrate her new book The Elegant Hustler: Adventures from a Life on the Move . From riding ocean waves to travelling on iconic railways across the world, the journeys may be long, but they are never dull – especially as drama seems to follow these two actors wherever they go!
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Death of Gesualdo: Concert Theatre Works & The Gesualdo Six
description: World renowned vocal ensemble The Gesualdo Six bring the chilling music and biography of composer Carlo Gesualdo to life in an exceptional multi-media concert directed by Bill Barclay with choreography by Will Tuckett. Following the success of Secret Byrd, Death of Gesualdo sees The Gesualdo Six collaborate once more with Bill Barclay’s Concert Theatre Works to tell the story of troubled madrigalist Carlo Gesualdo (1566 – 1613). Gesualdo may be most famous for murdering his wife and her lover in bed, but among composers he is revered for anticipating chromaticism by 200 years. 'Six actors join the singers on stage to tell Gesualdo’s dark, troubled story through puppetry, dance and tableaux vivants reminiscent of Renaissance paintings to a score of some of Gesualdo’s darkest music, including Tenebrae (a mass for the composer’s own death), performed with the Gesualdo Six’s renowned “supreme precision".' (Evening Standard) ‘The staging, singing and acting are sublime’ (The Times ★★★★★) Commissioned by The Church of St Martin-in-the-Fields, The National Centre for Early Music in York, and Music Before 1800 (NYC). Please be aware that this performance contains nudity, as well as depictions of themes including domestic violence, murder, and substance abuse. While the pristine sounds of this concert can be enjoyed throughout the venue, please note that side seats will have less direct views of some of the theatrical elements. This is reflected in the ticket prices.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Jess Gillam Trio
description: With her electrifying performances, vibrant stage presence and magnetic personality, celebrated saxophonist Jess Gillam has been invited to play on the world’s major stages since becoming the youngest ever soloist to perform at the Last Night of the Proms at the Royal Albert Hall. Her award-winning weekly show, This Classical Life , on BBC Radio 3 is now in its seventh season. As part of a trio with piano and bass, she weaves together a programme of fantastic lesser-known repertoire with fresh takes on some firm favourites. From jazz influences with music by composers such as Sidney Bechet and Barbara Thompson, to baroque energy from Corelli and Telemann, this programme has something for everyone to enjoy.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Kent College Lecture – Daniel Susskind: What Should My Children Do?
description: How should the next generation prepare for a world being transformed by artificial intelligence? AI is rewriting the rules in the classroom, the job market and the workplace, leaving parents, teachers, students and all types of workers uncertain about what lies ahead. Globally renowned economist Daniel Susskind has spent the past fifteen years exploring the impact of technology on our working lives. Now, he tackles the number one question on every parent’s mind with an evidence-based and practical guide to seizing opportunity in the age of AI.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Lindisfarne
description: Legendary 70s folk-rock pioneers Lindisfarne return to form with a classic five-piece line up of long-time members, fronted by original founding member Rod Clements on vocals, mandolin and slide guitar. With a repertoire of unforgettable songs like Meet Me On The Corner, Fog On The Tyne, Lady Eleanor and Run For Home and their trademark power to galvanise festival and concert, Lindisfarne are sure to get you on your feet and singing along. ‘More than half a century after their formation, Lindisfarne are… sounding better than ever’ (Louder Than War)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: London African Gospel Choir: Paul Simon's Graceland: 40th Anniversary Celebration
description: Prepare to be uplifted, inspired, and moved to your feet as the sensational London African Gospel Choir come to Canterbury with their critically acclaimed, soul-deep reinterpretation of one of the most beloved albums of all time: Paul Simon’s Graceland . This one-of-a-kind show sees the pan African choir — comprised of world-class vocalists, musicians and dancers from across Africa and the diaspora — breathe new life into the iconic 1986 album. Since their first sold-out performance at London’s Jazz Cafe, the London African Gospel Choir have wowed thousands across the globe including concerts at The Royal Albert Hall and Southbank Centre. Expect irresistible rhythms, soaring harmonies, joyous dance, and the spiritual power of gospel. ‘Extraordinary! Every song was a highlight… the musicianship and vocal power were incredible.’ (Songlines Magazine)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Love, Loss & Protest: Imogen Whitehead & Olivia Jageurs
description: Imogen Whitehead – Flugelhorn Olivia Jageurs – Harp Chaminade – Concertino Fauré – Chanson d’amour / Les roses d’Ispahan / Après un rêve Hildegard von Bingen – O virtus sapientiae Jean-Michel Damase – Adage JS Bach – Jesu, joy of man’s desiring Gavin Higgins – Alarms Traditional – O waly, waly Florence Price – Sympathy Lennon-McCartney – Blackbird Peter Maxwell Davies – Farewell to Stromness Rodgers & Hammerstein – The Sound of Music Two outstanding musicians invite you on a journey through songs of love, loss and protest, from the quiet faith of Bach’s Jesu, joy of man’s desiring to the defiance of The Beatles’ hit Blackbird and the famous Farewell to Stromness . Expressed through the distinctive colours of flugelhorn and harp, this music asks not only what we cherish and what we lose, but what we are prepared to stand for.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Martin James Bartlett Piano Recital
description: Bach (arr. Wilhelm Kempff) - Wachet auf, ruft uns die Stimme , BWV 645 Bach - Siciliano (from Flute Sonata in E-flat Major, BWV 1031) Handel - Chaconne in G Major, HWV 435 Mozart - Piano Sonata No. 11 in A Major, K. 331 Chopin - Nocturne in D-flat Major, Op. 27, No. 2 Fauré - Nocturne No. 6 in D-flat Major, Op. 63 Fauré - Sicilienne , Op. 78 Granados - Los Requiebros (from Goyescas , Op. 11) From early fame as a BBC Young Musician of the Year finalist in 2014 to being the inaugural recipient of the Prix Serdang in 2022, Martin’s performances are hailed as a thrilling experience delivered with passion and technical mastery. His festival appearance includes pieces by Bach, Handel, Mozart, Chopin and Fauré. Renowned for his fearless technique and musical maturity, pianist Martin James Bartlett has performed across the UK, USA and Europe with appearances at the Wigmore Hall, the Elbphilharmonie Hamburg, BBC Proms and collaborations with leading orchestras.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Pasadena Roof Orchestra: Swinging into Christmas
description: The Pasadena Roof Orchestra take to the stage with their joyful Christmas concert, Swinging Into Christmas! Don’t miss this festive musical extravaganza as the orchestra bring their roaring-twenties flair and British humour to our favourite seasonal classics, including Baby It’s Cold Outside, White Christmas, Winter Wonderland and more.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Paul Merton & Suki Webster’s Improv Show
description: Following critically acclaimed seasons in London and Edinburgh, Paul Merton and Suki Webster, two of the UK’s leading improvisers, present an evening of wonderfully unscripted cutting-edge comedy. Two of the UK’s masters of improvisation are joined by their old friend, improv virtuoso, Mike McShane and a host of very special guests for an evening of sensational improvised comedy. Expect fast and fabulously funny improvised games, scenes, stories, songs and laugh out loud surrealism. All deftly conjured up on the spot and based on suggestions by you: The Audience! ‘Merton and Webster have established themselves as two of the UK’s best…this show is improv comedy at its strongest.’ (★★★★ The Scots Reviewer) ‘They continue to be the finest exponents of the improv art.’ (The Mirror)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Ronnie Scott’s Jazz Club
description: Direct from London’s world-famous jazz club and combining world-class live jazz, narration, rare archive photos, and video footage, The Ronnie Scott’s All Stars take to the stage to celebrate The Ronnie Scott’s Story . Mixing classic music from the jazz greats who have performed at Ronnie Scott’s over the years, alongside tales of old Soho, miscreant musicians and police raids, this is a unique evening celebrating one of the world's most famous venues, its music, and history.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Ruby Turner
description: Jamaican-born soul and R&B powerhouse Ruby Turner is one of the UK’s leading soul singers. From her big break with Culture Club and her critically acclaimed debut album Women Hold Up Half the Sky — to performing with Jools Holland at the Queen's Diamond Jubilee Concert and being awarded an MBE, Ruby has established herself as a household name. To date, Ruby has released 17 albums including The Motown Songbook which saw her collaborate with legends of the era including The Four Tops, The Temptations and Jimmy Ruffin. A Festival favourite, Ruby returns with her band to the intimate setting of the Great Hall for a masterclass in soul.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Rumer: An Intimate Evening of Song
description: BRIT-nominated British singer-songwriter Rumer closes Canterbury Festival 2026 in style with her soulful tones, powerful voice and raw, frank lyrics. Hailed as ‘heir to Dusty Springfield and Karen Carpenter’ ( Q Magazine ), Rumer’s smoky, soothing tones saw her debut album Seasons of My Soul sell over a million copies worldwide. Since then she has established herself as a force to be reckoned with rave reviews for her live shows, paying homage to her inspirations and gaining praise from artists including Elton John and Burt Bacharach. From her debut through to her latest live album In Session , Rumer’s back catalogue is stacked with modern standards as well as favourites by Burt Bacharach, Hall and Oates and more. Don’t miss your chance to experience an evening of timeless music in this intimate setting.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Seth Lakeman
description: Seth Lakeman marks 20 years of his gold-selling album Freedom Fields with an intimate concert at this year’s Festival. His distinctive brand of indie-folk blends rich harmonies, fiery fiddle and compelling songwriting. Featuring favourites including Lady of the Sea , King and Country and White Hare , the album remains a standout in his catalogue, earning Album of the Year at the BBC Folk Awards. Expect an evening of captivating musicianship from one of folk’s most celebrated performers.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: The Bubble Show®
description: Step into a world of wonder with the internationally acclaimed bubble show. Created and performed by Guinness World Record holder, The Highland Joker, this theatrical family experience blends storytelling, magic, science, and bubble artistry into an unforgettable journey. From tiny bubbles to giant bubbles, from smoke bubbles to fire bubbles, and even a sparkling bubble cube and an erupting bubble volcano — every moment is full of colour, excitement, and interactive fun.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: The Glenn Miller Orchestra
description: Step back into the 1930s and ‘40s with the official Glenn Miller Orchestra in this joyful celebration of the legendary big band leader. Following sold-out shows at venues including London’s Barbican Hall and Liverpool Philharmonic Hall, the orchestra visit Canterbury for a concert packed with Miller’s most popular hits, including In the Mood , Moonlight Serenade and Little Brown Jug . Expect impeccable musicianship and infectious energy with director, conductor and veteran band leader Ray McVay at the helm.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: A Message for the Future
description: Aimed at ages 0 – 3, older siblings welcome A multi-sensory immersive installation and gig theatre experience designed for early years and family audiences. DIY punk poetry gig theatre parents Brigitte Aphrodite and Quiet Boy offer a joyfully different multi-sensory immersive experience with rock n roll storytelling, celebrating a journey of nature and kindness. This calm pocket of punky joy nurtures a sense of hopefulness and connection with our little ones and the environment whilst engaging multiple senses – sight, sound, touch, and smell. Created by Crescendorious with original storytelling and live music by acclaimed Margate gig theatre-makers Brigitte Aphrodite and Quiet Boy. Set design and costumes by internationally renowned set designer and concept artist Xander Lo Lo No, who has collaborated with Lady Gaga, Björk and MTV.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Ayanna Witter-Johnson
description: Singer, songwriter, cellist, composer and MOBO-nominee Ayanna Witter-Johnson is a captivating one-woman orchestra. Blending classical, jazz, reggae, soul and R&B, she performs with virtuosic flair, using her cello as both rhythmic anchor and emotional compass. A second-generation Jamaican born in Britain, her work celebrates her heritage, culture and identity through music that is both deeply rooted and boundlessly inventive. Ayanna has been championed by icons from Gilles Peterson to Jools Holland and has collaborated and toured with musical legends including Andrea Bocelli and Peter Gabriel. She releases New Roots , her first solo album in three years this September, imbuing her exploratory, soul-leaning sound with the colour of jazz and groundedness of folk storytelling.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Bring Your Own Baby
description: Bring Your Own Baby returns with 75 minutes of brilliant stand-up comedy, designed for parents who still want to catch a great stand-up act —no babysitter required. Enjoy top UK comedians in a relaxed, baby-friendly setting where laughter is guaranteed. The bar will be open for tea, coffee, or something a little stronger. And if the little ones need attention, that’s perfectly fine—whether it’s feeds, changes or baby babbling, everything is welcome. Even a bit of pint-sized heckling won’t faze our performers! Please note that we have a 15-month age limit for our little laughers, as our comedians will be performing their usual grown-up jokes! Best bring-along Baby Event (HOOP Awards) Best Mental Health Boost for New Parents 2022 (UK Enterprise Awards)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Drunk Women Solving Crime
description: A night of crime with the hit comedy podcast Drunk Women Solving Crime! After past sell out runs across the UK and the Edinburgh Fringe, hosts Hannah George and Taylor Glenn are taking to the road to record their award-winning show LIVE at Canterbury Festival. Come and be part of the experience — you'll get to see two episodes recorded as celebrity guests help "solve" a crime over a glass or two on stage. Plus audience members are given the chance to share their own tale for a prize. Past guests include Katherine Ryan, Romesh Ranganathan, Rosie Jones and James Acaster.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Jacqueline Wilson
description: Aimed at 5+, all ages welcome Celebrate the power of books and reading with beloved children's author Dame Jacqueline Wilson. Jacqueline has written over 100 books and created characters such as Tracy Beaker, Hetty Feather and now the wonderfully funny and resilient heroine of her latest book, Esme Pepper .
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Olivia Chaney
description: Grammy-nominated songwriter, pianist, guitarist and harmonium player Olivia Chaney brings her unmistakable brand of luscious and sophisticated folk to Canterbury. From the rave reviews of her first solo album The Longest River , to the 2024 release Circus of Desire and her latest single Dark Eyed Sailor , which rose to fame in the new Wuthering Heights film , Olivia’s perfectly crafted music is a spellbinding experience, exploring the clash of tradition and modernity, and the paradoxes of love. ‘Forget the labels, she is a major artist' (The Telegraph ★★★★★)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Out of the Box
description: Aimed at ages 3+, all welcome A joyful, family-friendly, comedy circus show packed with seriously skillful silliness for all ages. With every opening of the box comes a new surprise, including trick shots, mini acts, and hilarious opportunities for participation – but don’t worry – Darryl only ever wants you to leave the stage feeling like the star of the show (there’s no ridicule here!). Delivered through non-verbal clowning, ordinary objects become extraordinary – and you won’t believe the reaction he gets from a toothbrush in the finale!
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Screaming Alley Cabaret
description: This Halloween, Kent’s premier providers of radical cabaret, Screaming Alley bring a gloriously chaotic night of dark cabaret featuring drag, comedy, burlesque, dance and curious delights. Hosted by the much-loved Charlie Partridge and featuring drag king Pat Riarchy, poet provocateur Violet Malice, boylesque circus performer and pocket rocket Danny Ash, burlesque royalty Vicky Butterfly and cult cabaret collective The Screaming Marys, expect outrageous characters, filthy humour, wicked glamour and deliciously unhinged entertainment à la Screaming Alley. A late-October spectacular packed with mischief, mayhem and Halloween spirit from some of Kent and London’s most exciting underground performers.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Simon Watt: Look Up
description: Which planet is hottest? Which would float in a swimming pool? Which is the most studied? And just how much space is in space? Explore the science behind space exploration with scientist Simon Watt, see what experiments you can recreate at home or school, and learn about what you can see if you only look up!
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: The Mortal Immortal
description: Free, booking required Aimed at 10+, with support from an accompanying adult. Take part in a graphic novel workshop led by illustrator, Jon Schwochert and writers, Dr Susan Civale and Dr Rachel Stone, Enter the world of Mary Shelley’s The Mortal Immortal, where a young man meets a mad scientist with a magic potion promising eternal life. Bring this story to life through pictures. All you need is your imagination and creativity – we will provide the rest! Creative learning partner: Canterbury Christ Church University (English)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Tiddley Pom! Cabaret for the Modern Dystopia
description: Join Tom Carradine and Ida Barr, two of modern variety’s favourite purveyors of fun, for a new variety show packed with songs, stand-up, and silliness. Hosted by the pensioner Queen of Artificial Hip Hop, music hall singer turned rapper, Ida Barr, and featuring music director Tom Carradine’s Cockney sing-alongs, the evening showcases viral musical comedy star Thomas Benjamin Wild Esq, as well as impressive acrobatics and more. A joyful antidote to turbulent times, Tiddley Pom! is interactive, ridiculous, and ridiculously good fun.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: You’ll See
description: Aimed at 7+, all welcome. Combining live performance, intricate paper design, an original score and Joyce’s odyssey, this is theatre that will excite both young and old with its warmth, humour and beauty. James Joyce’s epic story of one day in one city is brought to life in this inventive show, bringing Ireland’s most notorious book to audiences aged eight and upwards, and to all those who haven’t got around to reading it yet. ‘Ulysses for children: has the world gone mad?... Branar has gone and done it.’ (The Irish Times)
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: MakeBeth
description: A playful, interactive, craft-along retelling of Shakespeare’s Macbeth for families and young audiences aged 7-11. Directed by Ian Nicholson and designed by multi award-winning Sam Wilde, MakeBeth bursts with live music, puppetry and plenty of hands-on creativity, transforming Shakespeare’s darkest tragedy into a joyful, laugh-out-loud, cardBARD adventure! Make the witches, become the moving trees of Birnam Wood, and even help Lady MacBeth with that damned spot! Makebeth is your chance to journey through the twists and turns of the Scottish play as they are brought to life using Cardboard Adventures’ trademark sustainable cardboard design. Don’t miss your chance to make-along with Shakespeare’s classic in this exciting interactive production from Cardboard Adventures and Shakespeare’s Globe in partnership with Chichester Festival Theatre and Leeds Playhouse. ‘It was wonderful, both kids and adults had a fantastic time!’ (Audience Member on Boxville) Written by Hannah Khalil. Aimed at ages 7 – 11, all welcome
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Ontroerend Goed presents Handle with Care
description: Handle with Care is a theatre performance in a box. No actors, no technicians. Just a box. And you. A box is mailed to the theatre. The instructions are clear: Invite a group of people, on a specific night, at a specific time. Let the audience take their seats. Place the box at the centre of the stage. The audience is waiting. And then an audience member stands up and opens the box. The show has started. In Handle with Care , renowned Belgian immersive theatre company Ontroerend Goed puts you in control. They provide the structure; you shape the experience. Choose your role — take the lead or observe as others make choices that steer the performance in unexpected directions. Together, you create something special. For one hour, live something unique, fleeting, and unrepeatable. What is yours, stays yours. Take good care of it. And send them a postcard.
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: Men Crowd to Listen
description: Aphra Behn (1640 – 1689), Canterbury’s daughter, was the first professional woman writer in English. Historian Breeze Barrington, author of The Graces , in conversation with Professor Elaine Hobby, explores the court circle of the 1680s, shedding light on how women like Aphra used literature, music and art to challenge men’s power – and how men pushed back. In partnership with the Aphra Behn Society
address: Canterbury Festival, Festival House, 8 Orange Street, Canterbury, CT1 2JA

title: The Circle & the Serpent: Harpsichord Recital
description: Harpsichordist Petra Hajduchová presents an evocative programme exploring the worlds of chaconnes and passacaglias with works by Fischer, Bach, Handel, and more in this intimate recital showcasing variation, virtuosity, and style across eighteenth-century Europe. Featuring imaginative transcriptions, Petra’s recital highlights the harpsichord’s expressive range. Petra is an acclaimed early music specialist who performs widely across the UK and Europe.

title: An Evening with Canterbury Catch Club
description: For almost a century (1779 – 1865) Canterbury took immense pride in its Catch Club, a regular night of music, song and wit in a room at the Prince of Wales tavern. In true Catch Club style, Dr Christopher Price and his talented musicians lead us through the history of the club, its 200-year-old lithograph depicting its members (on their best behaviour) and invite you to partake in some serious fun which was the hallmark of this immensely popular culture. The Parrot is a distinguished substitute for the Prince of Wales, and will serve a finger buffet which is included in the ticket price.
